top_findings.summary: Detected in all three files. The code exhibits template-based structure with placeholder comments ("Add processing logic here"), deceptive file extension (.sh file containing Python code in `aggregate_logs.sh`), and significant mismatch between described functionality (machine learning anomaly detection) and actual implementation (basic file I/O). This pattern is consistent with malicious skill frameworks that use generic templates to bypass initial security reviews before injecting actual malicious payloads. The recursive file traversal (`rglob('*')`) capability could be weaponized for data collection if combined with exfiltration logic in future modifications.
